subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

cases.passage: The development of the periodic table by Dmitri Mendeleev is a testament to the power of classification in chemistry. In the late 19th century, Mendeleev was working on a project to organize the known elements. He arranged the elements in a tabular form, where elements with similar properties and atomic weights were placed in the same group. This classification allowed him to predict the existence of undiscovered elements and their properties. For instance, he predicted the existence of elements like gallium and scandium, which were later discovered. This demonstrates the importance of classification in understanding the periodic trends of elements.

questions.q: What is the primary reason behind Dmitri Mendeleev's development of the periodic table?
opts: To understand the properties of elements
(B) To predict the existence of undiscovered elements
(C) To categorize elements based on their reactivity
(D) To study the structure of atoms
questions.explanation: Mendeleev's development of the periodic table was primarily to predict the existence of undiscovered elements and their properties.

questions.q: Which of the following elements was predicted by Mendeleev and later discovered?
questions.explanation: Gallium was predicted by Mendeleev and later discovered in 1875.

questions.q: What is the basis of classification in Mendeleev's periodic table?
questions.explanation: Mendeleev's periodic table is based on the atomic weights of elements.

questions.q: What is one of the advantages of the periodic table developed by Mendeleev?
opts: It helps in predicting the properties of elements
(B) It helps in categorizing elements based on their reactivity
(C) It helps in understanding the structure of atoms
(D) It helps in studying the chemical reactions
questions.explanation: One of the advantages of Mendeleev's periodic table is that it helps in predicting the properties of elements.

questions.q: Who developed the periodic table of elements?
questions.explanation: Dmitri Mendeleev is credited with the development of the periodic table of elements.

cases.passage: The chemical properties of elements in the same group of the periodic table show a periodic trend. For instance, the elements in Group 1 (alkali metals) are highly reactive, whereas those in Group 17 (halogens) are less reactive. This trend is due to the fact that elements in the same group have the same number of electrons in their outermost shell. As a result, they exhibit similar chemical properties. However, the properties of elements in the same period show a gradual change. For example, the elements in Period 3 (sodium, magnesium, aluminum) exhibit a decrease in reactivity due to the increase in atomic number.

questions.q: Why do elements in the same group of the periodic table show similar chemical properties?
opts: Because they have the same atomic number
(B) Because they have the same number of electrons in their outermost shell
(C) Because they have similar atomic weights
(D) Because they are located in the same period
questions.explanation: Elements in the same group have the same number of electrons in their outermost shell, which leads to similar chemical properties.

questions.q: Which group of elements in the periodic table is highly reactive?
questions.explanation: Elements in Group 1 (alkali metals) are highly reactive.

questions.q: Why do elements in the same period exhibit a gradual change in properties?
opts: Because they have the same number of electrons in their outermost shell
(B) Because they have similar atomic weights
(C) Because they exhibit a decrease in atomic number
(D) Because they have an increase in atomic number
questions.explanation: Elements in the same period exhibit a gradual change in properties due to an increase in atomic number.

questions.q: What is the trend observed in the reactivity of elements in Period 3?
opts: (D) An increase then decrease in reactivity
questions.explanation: The reactivity of elements in Period 3 decreases due to an increase in atomic number.

questions.q: What is the reason behind the similarity in chemical properties of elements in the same group?
opts: (C) Same number of electrons in outermost shell
questions.explanation: Elements in the same group have the same number of electrons in their outermost shell, which leads to similar chemical properties.
